title: Payments API idempotency keys

blockers.title: Atomic dedup
blockers.body: SET NX claim with an in-progress sentinel that completes only after a durable charge result; without it two concurrent same-key requests both miss the cache and double-charge.

blockers.title: Request-fingerprint binding
blockers.body: Bind key -> hash(request); a reused key with a different payload returns 409/422, never a replayed or unrelated charge.

blockers.title: Redis failure policy + kill switch
blockers.body: Redis is now in the critical payment path; decide fail-open vs fail-closed explicitly, with a kill switch.

blockers.title: Never cache transient failures
blockers.body: Do not store 5xx/timeouts as authoritative; a cached 500 poisons the key and blocks legitimate retries for 24h.

blockers.title: Replay fidelity
blockers.body: Replay status code, body, and headers consistently.

blockers.title: Phased rollout + observability
blockers.body: Dark-launch then cohort/percentage ramp; alert on Redis latency and cache-hit rate. Replace the big-bang enablement.

dissent.body: "All clients at once" is not inherently impossible given optional keys and backwards-compatible server behavior - but only with a kill switch, metrics, and staged enablement by route/client cohort. The rollout aggressiveness is debatable; the correctness blockers are not.

open_questions: 24h TTL vs client retry/settlement windows - does a legitimate retry after expiry double-charge?
Alignment with the downstream payment processor's own idempotency guarantees.
Dispute/audit retention needs vs the 24h window.
next_actions: Spec the atomic state machine + fingerprint.
Decide Redis failure policy + kill switch.
